file_insights.changes: Complete rewrite from 68-line ASCII terminal demo to 400-line storage resilience specification
key_sections: Summary of multi-layered storage corruption defense
4 corruption scenarios with detection and mitigation strategies
JSONL audit trail design (meta_written and stage_transition events)
Blake3 checksum validation algorithm for read/write paths
Storage format decision rationale (filesystem+WAL vs SQLite/Dolt)
file_insights.content_characteristics: Technical specification with code examples, JSON schemas, and bash commands

patterns_discovered.context: write_meta() uses fs::write to temp file then fs::rename to target, eliminating partial-write corruption window
patterns_discovered.evidence: Scenario 1 mitigation code example shows this pattern preventing mid-flight crashes

patterns_discovered.pattern: Blake3 checksum validation on read path
patterns_discovered.context: read_meta() strips checksum, reserializes to canonical form, recomputes hash, and validates match
patterns_discovered.evidence: Detailed code path shows serialize→hash→validate flow with hex encoding

patterns_discovered.context: JSONL event append uses `let _ = append_event(...)` to never abort state transitions on I/O failure
patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple references to non-fatal WAL write failures in dispatcher

patterns_discovered.context: Always use serde_json::to_vec (compact), never to_vec_pretty, to eliminate whitespace ambiguity
patterns_discovered.evidence: Implementation details section explains whitespace causes checksum mismatch between write and verify

patterns_discovered.pattern: Filesystem state machine with event log audit trail
patterns_discovered.context: fs::rename atomic transitions paired with JSONL events for post-mortem analysis
patterns_discovered.evidence: Race condition scenario shows how event log reveals simultaneous writes from different pids

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Adapter process killed during meta.json write leaves partial JSON on disk
gotchas_discovered.impact: Card becomes unreadable by dispatcher without tmp+rename atomicity
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: fs::rename provides POSIX guarantee: old file OR new file, never both

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Malicious/buggy adapter bypasses write_meta() and directly overwrites meta.json with valid but wrong data
gotchas_discovered.impact: Card appears 'done' but work never completed; semantic corruption not caught by JSON parser
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Blake3 checksum invalidates any modification; dispatcher detects and refuses processing

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Whitespace in JSON serialization causes checksum mismatch between write and verify
gotchas_discovered.impact: Write with pretty-print spaces, read without spaces → valid JSON but hash fails mysteriously
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always use compact JSON (to_vec), document this as canonical form requirement

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: JSONL event log not atomic for multi-event operations (e.g. both meta_written and stage_transition)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Race condition where adapter writes after dispatcher transitions leaves orphaned writes in log
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: WAL is best-effort observability only; state machine correctness relies on fs::rename, not log

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: jj workspace COW cloning does not preserve shared target/ artifacts across workspaces
gotchas_discovered.impact: Each card rebuilds everything instead of sharing compiler IR via APFS COW
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Seed target/ with `cp -c -R workspace1/target workspace2/target` before build

approach_outcome.deliverable: output/result.md written with full corruption scenario analysis
coverage: 4 corruption vectors documented (mid-write crashes, malicious writes, filesystem corruption, race conditions)
Mitigations for each scenario with code examples and rationale
JSONL WAL schema with event types and field definitions
Storage format decision closed with SQLite/Dolt rejection rationale
Blake3 implementation details with canonical JSON requirement
Unit and integration test coverage listed
Operations manual for corruption detection and recovery

recommendations.title: Enforce compact JSON serialization across all write paths
recommendations.rationale: Whitespace in JSON causes checksum verification to fail even though data is correct; document this as non-negotiable

recommendations.title: Add blake3 checksum dependency and integrate into read_meta()
recommendations.rationale: Specification is complete but code implementation pending; checksum field must be populated on write and validated on read

recommendations.title: Test checksum validation with deliberately corrupted meta.json files
recommendations.rationale: Unit tests should include scenarios where checksum field is modified, metadata is corrupted, or data is truncated

recommendations.title: Monitor event logs in production for `meta_written` events that occur after `stage_transition`
recommendations.rationale: This pattern indicates race conditions between dispatcher and adapters; use for telemetry/alerting

recommendations.title: Document backward compatibility for cards without checksum field
recommendations.rationale: Spec notes graceful degradation; ensure migration strategy for existing cards in the field

recommendations.title: Implement bop recover subcommand for automated JSONL replay
recommendations.rationale: Recovery section describes `bop recover <card>` but implementation is not yet confirmed

recommendations.title: Consider sparse JSONL rotation for long-running cards
recommendations.rationale: Event logs are append-only; no truncation strategy documented for cards that live months
